title: PATCH /api/v1/accounts/update_credentials — profile field editing
description:
## What
Implement PATCH /api/v1/accounts/update_credentials to update display name, bio, locked flag, bot flag, and fields array. HTML-sanitize bio with ammonia. Return full Account object.

## Why
Core of profile management (sub-epic 8-A). Without this, clients cannot edit any profile information. Ivory, Elk, and Phanpy all call this endpoint on first login settings flow.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: fetch mastodon.social /api/v1/accounts/update_credentials live response shape (OPTIONS + PATCH with test account), document all accepted fields and their types, check fields_attributes[] multipart encoding format.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: add the axum handler for PATCH /api/v1/accounts/update_credentials; accept display_name, note (bio markdown), locked, bot, discoverable, fields_attributes[N][name]/[value]; render bio through pulldown-cmark then ammonia sanitize; store rendered HTML in bio_html column and raw markdown in bio column; update accounts row; serialize and return full Account object.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration tests against an in-memory SQLite DB with a seeded account; verify bio HTML rendering from markdown; verify XSS is stripped by ammonia; verify fields_attributes round-trips correctly; verify locked/bot flags update; verify response shape matches mastodon.social fixture.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: curl mastodon.social /api/v1/accounts/update_credentials response shape (not the code under test); ammonia default allowlist documented in ammonia crate README.
- Test approach: integration test with real in-memory SQLite (not mocked); happy path (update all fields), edge cases: empty fields_attributes clears array, note with `<script>alert(1)</script>` is stripped, note with `javascript:` href is stripped, fields_attributes with 5 items is rejected (max 4).

## Defensive programming checklist
- Reject fields_attributes with more than 4 items (Mastodon limit)
- HTML-sanitize bio with ammonia using default tag/attr allowlist — do not trust raw input
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths; all DB errors map to AppError
- Return 422 for malformed multipart or type errors
- Return 200 with full Account object on success (not 204)
